segments.title: Austro-Hungarian destroyers jump to the Tatra class
segments.summary: The lecture frames the Tatra class as a sharp improvement over the conservative Huszar destroyers, with better guns, torpedoes, speed, range, and industrial backing.
segments.body: The discussion captures the design-development pivot in the episode. Dr. Clarke explains that the Huszar class was built as destroyers but was very small and conservative by the time the final ships entered service, then contrasts that with the Tatra class: Skoda 100 mm guns, twin torpedo mountings, higher speed, useful range, and construction through Ganz-Danubius.

evidence.note: The transcript says the Tatra class was built because the Huszars were saturated and frames the earlier design as conservative but still destroyers.
The armament section identifies the Tatra class's Skoda 100 mm guns, secondary guns, and twin 450 mm torpedo tubes.
The closing specification window gives the larger crew, 32.5-knot speed, 1,600-nautical-mile range, and Ganz-Danubius construction.
